36th Annual Conference
Reorienting Towards Hope
📅  16–19 April 2026 📍  Loutraki, Greece 👥  ~155 participants
About the conference

Across societies, workplaces, and communities, many people feel a growing sense of uncertainty. Economic disruption, social fragmentation, environmental crises, and political polarisation have created a climate in which anxiety often overshadows possibility.

Hope can easily be misunderstood — reduced to optimism or wishful thinking. Yet genuine hope is something far deeper. It emerges when people see reality clearly, reconnect with sources of meaning, and act together to shape a better future.

Rather than offering easy answers, this conference creates a space for meaningful dialogue and collective learning, drawing on concrete experiences from business, civil society, and community initiatives.

The learning journey
1
Seeing reality clearly
2
Discovering sources of hope
3
Calibrating the inner compass
4
Learning about practices grounded in hope
5
Building communities that sustain hope
The inquiry across four days
Thursday

What does it mean to reorient toward hope?

Friday

What makes such reorientation possible in reality?

Saturday

What does acting from this orientation require?

Sunday

What will I now reorient in my life or work?

Orientation emerges from: the assumptions we hold about reality · the experiences that have shaped us · the identity we carry · and the purpose we believe our lives serve.
